final_answer: To reduce the risk of getting COVID-19, individuals should avoid crowded places and practice physical distancing, keeping at least 1 metre distance from others [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Wearing a mask in public places where community transmission is present and physical distancing is not possible is also recommended [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Frequent handwashing with soap and water or an alcohol-based hand rub is crucial [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Keeping indoor spaces well ventilated helps reduce the risk of virus transmission [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00016]. Staying informed about the latest COVID-19 guidance from local authorities is important [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00029]. Getting vaccinated can help reduce the spread of the virus and its mutations [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00012]. Regularly cleaning and disinfecting frequently touched surfaces is also advised [doc3_covid19.txt:chunk_00026]. Finally, testing for COVID-19 if you have symptoms or after exposure to the virus is essential [doc2_covid19.txt:chunk_00023].
